tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals marked proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), all suggestive of nephrotic syndrome or advanced kidney disease. Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, making diabetes unlikely. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
